preamble: The nasalized close-mid front unrounded vowel /ɛ̃/ can undergo a phonetic transformation involving both denasalization and lowering, resulting in the near-open front unrounded vowel /æ/. This shift is observed in the historical evolution of certain languages and in the adaptation of loanwords.

examples.note: In French loanwords, the nasalized close-mid front unrounded vowel /ɛ̃/ can be denasalized and lowered to the near-open front unrounded vowel /æ/.

The historical evolution of the nasal vowel /ɛ̃/ in Standard French has shown a tendency towards a more open pronunciation, often realized as [æ̃] (a nasalized near-open front unrounded vowel), indicating a lowering effect while retaining nasality.

sources: Sakr, G. (March 06 2017). A Discussion of Issues from French Loans in Lebanese.
French Language Stack Exchange. (June 04 2019). Why is the nasal vowel /ɛ̃/ transcribed as it is if it is closer to [ã]?.
University of York. nasalization as universal phonological process.
